story: A Wood River pilot is training an AI computer-vision model on automated drone imagery to count Bristol Bay sockeye escapement, tested in the 2026 season against ADF&G counting towers used since 1955.

claims.claim: An autonomous drone-in-a-box launches at 8:30 a.m. on an automated flight to an observation position above the right bank of the Wood River.
claims.verbatim: At exactly 8:30 a.m., the box opens and a drone emerges. It quickly takes off on an automated flight path, flying to an observation position above the right bank of the Wood River.

claims.claim: The drone imagery is fed into a computer model intended to generate more accurate salmon escapement counts.
claims.verbatim: feed the images into a computer model to generate more accurate salmon escapement counts
claims.notes: Framed as a goal/expectation; soften as 'meant to' or 'intended to' produce more accurate counts. Accuracy is not yet demonstrated.

claims.claim: Machine-learning developer Ian Chiu describes the end product as a computer vision model trained to pick out salmon from video.
claims.verbatim: The end product is that we'll be able to feed in a video, and then we'll have a computer vision model that will be trained on picking out salmon.
claims.notes: Verbatim quote confirmed identically on both KDLG and KMXT pages.

claims.claim: The team will likely need several thousand drone images to train the model to reliably identify a fish; technicians hand-label the imagery.
claims.verbatim: they'll likely need several thousand drone images for the model to learn how to reliably identify a fish
claims.notes: Soften as 'likely' / 'expected to need' — the figure is an estimate.

claims.claim: Alaska Department of Fish and Game has used counting towers for Bristol Bay escapement since 1955 (70+ years).
claims.notes: Page states towers used 'since 1955'; '70-year' framing follows arithmetically for 2026.

claims.claim: Tower crews rotate through continuous 24-hour shifts, counting each passing salmon with a hand-clicker in 10-minute intervals.
claims.verbatim: rotate through continuous shifts over 24-hours, counting each salmon that swims by with a hand-clicker in 10 minute intervals

claims.claim: Most Bristol Bay escapement counts currently come from nine Fish & Game counting tower sites.
claims.verbatim: Most escapement counts across the Bay currently come from nine Fish & Game counting tower sites.

claims.claim: Project partners include the University of Washington's Fisheries Research Institute (lab at Lake Aleknagik), the Bristol Bay Regional Seafood Development Association, and the Bristol Bay Economic Development Corporation.
claims.verbatim: University of Washington's Fisheries Research Institute ... Lake Aleknagik ... Bristol Bay Regional Seafood Development Association ... Bristol Bay Economic Development Corporation
claims.notes: All four partner references appear on the page.

claims.claim: At this stage, Alaska Department of Fish and Game is not directly involved in the drone project.
claims.verbatim: At this stage, Fish & Game isn't directly involved in the project
claims.notes: IMPORTANT: do NOT frame ADF&G as a partner. The pilot is tested against ADF&G's independent tower counts, but the agency is not a participant at this stage.

claims.claim: Project lead Norm Van Vactor says he has been engaged in the Bristol Bay commercial fishery for 40 years.
claims.verbatim: I've been engaged and involved in the commercial fishery here for 40 years.

claims.claim: Ian Chiu develops the machine-learning components of the project remotely.
claims.verbatim: Ian Chiu ... works remotely developing machine learning
claims.notes: Role confirmed as remote ML developer; no formal title on the page.

claims.claim: Van Vactor calls the moment an 'amazing convergence of technology,' citing drone-in-a-box, machine learning and AI, Starlink Mini, lithium batteries and solar panels all maturing in the last year or two.
claims.verbatim: We currently have this amazing convergence of technology. This drone in the box technology combined with machine learning and AI combined with things like Starlink Mini combined with lithium batteries and solar panels it's all happening just in the last year or two, and it's all coming together to make something like this possible.
claims.notes: Source contains an en/em dash before 'it's all happening'; house style forbids dashes, so render as two sentences or a comma when quoting on a slide. Also sources the enabling tech list.

claims.claim: The 2026 Bristol Bay preseason forecast estimates a total run of 44.05 million sockeye.
claims.verbatim: The forecast this year estimates a total run of 44.05 million sockeye.

claims.claim: As of July 6, 2026, the total Bristol Bay sockeye run to date was 26,944,183 fish.
claims.verbatim: Escapement yesterday was 737,596 fish, bringing the total run so far to 26,944,183 fish.
claims.notes: As-of date is the July 6, 2026 report.

claims.claim: As of the July 6, 2026 report, Wood River tower escapement to date totaled 2,112,834 fish.
claims.verbatim: making their total count 2,112,834 fish, plus another 18,264 sockeye past the tower as of 6 a.m. this morning.

claims.claim: Wood River tower crews counted 57,258 sockeye on Sunday.
claims.verbatim: The Wood River tower crews counted 57,258 sockeye on Sunday
claims.notes: Single-day count from the July 6 report.

claims.claim: The Wood River escapement goal range is 700,000 to 2.8 million fish, and the 2026 run is within that range.
claims.verbatim: they are within their escapement goal range of 700,000 to 2.8 million fish.

claims.claim: The story was written by Jessie Sheldon and republished statewide via KMXT on July 8, 2026 (originally KDLG, July 3, 2026).
claims.verbatim: Jessie Sheldon ... July 8, 2026 at 4:00 PM AKDT

docket_claims.claim: Alaska DNR received more than 500 public comments on Stak Energy's proposed North Slope data-center land lease, with fewer than a dozen in favor.
docket_claims.value: 500+ comments; fewer than a dozen in favor
docket_claims.verbatim: More than 500 comments before preliminary deadline ... Fewer than a dozen endorsed the project
docket_claims.notes: Double-sourced with ADN 2026-07-07 and Alaska Beacon.

docket_claims.claim: DNR extended the written-comment deadline by about a month to July 17, 2026.
docket_claims.verbatim: New deadline: July 17 (original was mid-June) ... Extension period: One additional month
docket_claims.notes: The '4:30 p.m. ADT' time could NOT be verified on fetched news pages; state the date without the time.

docket_claims.claim: DNR spokesperson Sean Clifton attributed the extension to the volume of comments, public interest and requests for extension.
docket_claims.verbatim: the volume of comments received, public interest and requests for extension

docket_claims.claim: Stak Energy chief strategy officer John Boyle said most comments are form letters lacking substance.
docket_claims.verbatim: Our initial assessment is that the vast majority of the comments are form letters lacking substance other than reflecting an individual's point of view

docket_claims.claim: GVEA's third-quarter (effective June 1, 2026) fuel and purchased-power charge rose about 61 percent, adding nearly $46 a month for the average residential member.
docket_claims.verbatim: will go up by about 61 percent on June 1st ... that'll cost the average residential nearly $46 more per month
docket_claims.notes: The exact '20.652 cents/kWh' rate and 'record' framing could NOT be verified (GVEA release 403). Do not assert them.

docket_claims.claim: The GVEA board is set to weigh, in executive session on July 13, 2026, purchasing a new LM6000 unit costing about $120 million.
docket_claims.verbatim: the board will meet in executive session on July 13 ... about $120 million for a new unit.
docket_claims.notes: Frame as 'set to weigh/decide,' not a completed purchase.

docket_claims.claim: GVEA board member Mary Burtness questioned the co-op's continued dependence on fossil fuels given the turbine's price tag.
docket_claims.verbatim: It's a big price tag. Also, do we want to continue our dependence on a volatile and always-increasing price of fossil fuels?

dropped.reason: Conflicts with fetched July 6 fisheries report (44.05 million). Superseded by c13.

dropped.claim: 2026 field season June 8 to July 8; BBRSDA hired two seasonal drone technicians.
dropped.reason: bbrsda.com hiring post rendered blank on every fetch; no fetched page supports the dates or the count. KDLG only says 'these last two weeks.'

dropped.claim: GVEA Q3 rate 20.652 cents/kWh, largest increase in co-op history.
dropped.reason: GVEA release 403; exact cents/kWh and 'record' framing not on fetched KUAC/News-Miner pages. Only ~61% and ~$46/month verifiable (d5).

dropped.claim: STAK deadline specifically 4:30 p.m. ADT July 17, 2026.
dropped.reason: No fetched news page states the time; only the date is confirmed (d2).

dropped.claim: ADF&G provides comparison data / is a project partner.
dropped.reason: Contradicted by fetched KDLG: 'Fish & Game isn't directly involved in the project' (c9).
Not part of the selected story; single medium-credibility source, not re-verified. Excluded from this deck's factual base.
